chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 817 - وَ سَأَلَ يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ عَلَيْهِ اَلْبُرْطُلَةُ فَقَالَ «لاَ يَضُرُّهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.817 - Yunus ibn Ya'qub asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man praying while wearing a burṭulah (a type of hat or head covering).
The Imam said: "It does not harm him (there is no issue)."
